publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Actionable issues; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Actionable issues: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? A short title such as login broken is enough because developers can discover the environment and expected behavior themselves.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Provide concise reproducible evidence and testable acceptance boundaries so another person can confirm and close the work without guessing intent.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Provide concise reproducible evidence and testable acceptance boundaries so another person can confirm and close the work without guessing intent.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Actionable issues instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Provide concise reproducible evidence and testable acceptance boundaries so another person can confirm and close the work without guessing intent.
A short title such as login broken is enough because developers can discover the environment and expected behavior themselves.

answer.explanation: Provide concise reproducible evidence and testable acceptance boundaries so another person can confirm and close the work without guessing intent.
